prompt:
The usage export hit 2.3M rows last month and the dashboard worker OOMed building the whole report in memory before writing a single byte. I need two additions to the report package, and the ops guys wrote acceptance tests (stream_test.go) for exactly what they want:

Streaming: NewStream(w, columns, flushEvery) writing CSV straight to an io.Writer. Header goes out immediately at construction. Rows buffer in memory and get flushed to w every flushEvery rows — nothing in between, we're rate-limiting writes to a slow NFS mount. Close() flushes whatever is left and further Adds must fail (second Close is a harmless no-op). Same row semantics as Report.Add: declared columns only, missing keys are empty cells, standard quoting. flushEvery < 1 or a bad column list is a constructor error.

Subsets: r.Subset("email", "id") gives a new independent Report with just those columns in that order, existing rows projected. Asking for a column that doesn't exist fails.

Both new APIs should report unknown columns via a sentinel: errors.Is(err, ErrUnknownColumn). Current Report behavior is load-bearing for the dashboard, so report_test.go has to keep passing untouched, and don't edit stream_test.go either. Verify: go test -race -timeout 30s ./...
